{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Duct joints are unsealed",
    "Cloth duct tape observed on joints instead of code-required sealant (mastic or foil tape)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove all cloth duct tape",
    "Clean joint surfaces",
    "Apply UL 181A/B listed mastic or approved foil tape to all transverse and longitudinal joints",
    "Request re-inspection before insulation or concealment"
  ],
  "rationale": "IMC 603.9 requires duct joints and seams to be securely fastened and sealed with approved sealants. Cloth duct tape is not an approved sealant and will fail prematurely. SMACNA standards also mandate proper sealing for commercial ductwork to meet leakage limits. Manual D does not override these prescriptive code and standard requirements. The installation is non-compliant and defective as-is.",
  "references": [
    "IMC 603.9",
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standard (seal classes)",
    "UL 181A/B listing requirements for duct sealants"
  ]
}
